title: caucalis anthriscus
intro: Caucalis anthriscus, also known as wild chervil or pepperwort, is a plant species in the carrot family with hollow stems and small white flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as coumarins and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Caucalis anthriscus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support immune function. Caucalis anthriscus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
